hypothesis: For datasets with heavy-tailed graph size distributions (such as AFLOW), a hybrid batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching based on an online estimate of the current batch's coefficient of variation in node/edge counts will achieve lower mean training time per step than either algorithm alone, because it exploits static-64's faster batching overhead during epochs where sampled graphs cluster near the mean while avoiding costly recompilations during epochs where outlier graphs are encountered.
